_comment: SIMULATED / DUMMY DATA — not EmpowHER-303 results. All values are illustrative and were generated to demonstrate an interactive survival-extrapolation report. See README. This file holds the *parameters* of the model (medians, tail targets, anchors, diagnostics); the survival curves themselves are computed at runtime in app.js from these parameters so the dashboard stays small and data-driven.

OS.label: Overall survival (OS)
OS.note: Most immature endpoint — KM ends earliest, so the tail is the most model-dependent.

PFS.label: Progression-free survival (PFS)
PFS.note: Most mature endpoint — KM extends furthest, so extrapolation risk is lowest.

ToT.label: Time on treatment (ToT)
ToT.note: Treatment-discontinuation endpoint — used to inform drug-cost duration in the economic model.

None.label: None
None.note: full treatment effect maintained across the extrapolation

Hazard convergence.label: Hazard convergence
Hazard convergence.note: arm hazards converge gradually after the data cut

No benefit after progression.label: No benefit after progression
No benefit after progression.note: treatment effect is removed in the tail; arms merge

Jointly fitted (treatment as covariate).label: Jointly fitted (treatment as covariate)
Jointly fitted (treatment as covariate).note: treatment entered as a single covariate; the experimental curve is a constrained (proportional) transform of the control curve, so the arms stay closer in the tail

Fully stratified (separate per arm).label: Fully stratified (separate per arm)
Fully stratified (separate per arm).note: each arm fitted independently; the experimental tail is free to diverge from the control, giving a wider long-term separation

genPopFloor._comment: General-population mortality floor — very slow decline. The extrapolated tail must not cross below this in a plausible model.
